ALL SPECIALTIES / DENTAL ASSISTANT / NEVADA

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Nevada Dental Assistant Registration

Nevada Dental Assistant license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-03-22 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Dental Assistant applications in Nevada run through Nevada State Board of Dental Examiners. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Proof of Training - Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
  • DANB Certification - Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
  • Radiography Certification - Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 4 to 6 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Dental assistants can apply for Dental Assistant / EFDA through the Nevada State Board of Dental Examiners by license.

  1. STEP 1

    Review Expanded Function Requirements

    Determine which specific expanded function certification (e.g., Radiology, Coronal Polishing, Restorative Functions) you wish to obtain and review its unique requirements on the Nevada State Board of Dental Examiners website, including coursework, experience, and examinations.

    Fee: $200 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Gather Supporting Documentation

    Collect all necessary documents, which may include official transcripts, certificates of completion for approved courses, proof of current CPR certification, and any other specific requirements for your chosen expanded function.

    Fee: $200 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Create Online Portal Account

    Register for an account on the Nevada State Board of Dental Examiners Online Services Portal if you do not already have one. You will need to provide personal information and verify your email address.

    Fee: $200 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Complete and Submit Application

    Log in to the Nevada State Board of Dental Examiners Online Services Portal, complete the Expanded Functions Dental Assistant Application, upload all required supporting documents, and pay the non-refundable application fee for each certification requested.

    Fee: $200 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Board Review and Approval

    After submitting your complete application and all required documentation, the Nevada State Board of Dental Examiners will review your submission. You will be notified of the Board's decision regarding your certification.

    Fee: $200 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    Receive Certification

    Upon approval, your Expanded Functions Dental Assistant certification will be issued, allowing you to perform the approved procedures in Nevada.

    Fee: $200 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$200
Background check
Varies by provider
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$200

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
Nevada State Board of Dental Examiners
Phone
(702) 486-7070
Website
dental.nv.gov/
Application page
Open application

Renewal

Dental Assistant renewal in Nevada follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years
Renewal fee
$50
Continuing education
12 hours per renewal cycle

Frequently Asked Questions

Does Nevada require a dental assistant license?

Yes, Nevada requires Dental Assistant / EFDA through the state board.

Is DANB certification recognized in Nevada?

Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Nevada.

Can I take dental x-rays in Nevada?

Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Nevada?

State application fees are $200. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$200

Processing time

4 to 6 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

See board

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Every 2 years · $50

Nevada State Board of Dental Examiners
